sections.paragraph: συναπάγω [σύν, ἀπάγω] ‘carry off/away together with’, pass. in imagery of being so impressed that one is hoodwinked along with others by misrepresentation Gal 2:13 or by deceptive divergence from a standard 2 Pt 3:17. In Ro 12:16 the imagery is set in a positive context of being impressed by what is humble or lowly.
